title: lasthenia uliginosa
intro: Lasthenia uliginosa, also known as Marsh Goldfields or Marsh Rayless Daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Pacific coast of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds. The health benefits of Lasthenia uliginosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of allergies, skin conditions, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. Herbal preparations of Lasthenia uliginosa are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using a tincture or essential oil extract for topical applications.
